greenness.status: deferred (sign-validity): raw MODIS EVI dry-season anomalies in the Amazon are confounded by the sun-sensor-geometry 'green-up' artifact (Morton et al. 2014, Nature 506:221) and move the WRONG way under drought here; a geometry/BRDF-corrected greenness (MAIAC EVI / NIRv) is the upgrade. The MODIS loader is committed; the raw series is kept for audit, not rendered as stress.

sources.evi: MODIS MOD13Q1 EVI (public domain, ORNL subset) - deferred (sign-validity)

csd_status: deferred: the critical-slowing-down / resilience layer ships only after its false-alarm rate is characterized (surrogate null test, detrend and window sensitivity)
_caption: Observed source-forest condition over the box, as anomalies vs climatology. This describes condition; it does not forecast the harvest.
_note: Descriptive layer beside the static price. No coupling into f_loss, w_soybelt, A_source_ha, or the USD/ha.

et_crossval.method: anomaly-only, per-calendar-month z vs 2003-2020; box-representative means; ASO=Aug-Oct; correlation reported under multiple pre-stated rules (mean, median, all-pairs, holdout ex-2023/24, pre-event); drought-year agreement over <= 6 events (an agreement count and spread, never a p-value or calibrated probability)

sign_corroboration.note: the robust part: every paradigm, including the independent satellite leg, shows a negative dry-season anomaly in both 2023 and 2024. This is sign agreement on the event, not year-to-year correlation.

robustness_rules.note: the mean can be carried by one non-independent model-model pair, and the all-years value is inflated by the 2023/24 events; robust also requires the strictest pair and a holdout excluding 2023/24. Correlation that clears only the lenient mean is reported as partly corroborated, never robust.
